ddls:
CREATE TABLE "countries" (
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each country */,
  "country_name" TEXT /* Name of the country (e.g., France) */,
  "country_code" TEXT /* ISO Code of the country (e.g., FR) */,
  "region" TEXT /* Geopolitical region of the country */,
  "capital" TEXT /* Capital city of the country */,
  "population" INTEGER /* Population of the country */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("country_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "indicators" (
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic indicator */,
  "indicator_name" TEXT /* Name of the indicator (e.g., Inflation Rate) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the indicator */,
  "unit" TEXT /* Unit of measurement for the indicator (e.g., %) */,
  "calculation_method" TEXT /* Method used to calculate the indicator */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("indicator_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_sources" (
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data source */,
  "source_name" TEXT /* Name of the data source (e.g., World Bank) */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the data source */,
  "website" TEXT /* Website of the data source */,
  "data_update_frequency" TEXT /* Frequency at which data is updated (e.g., quarterly) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("source_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "economic_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each economic data entry */,
  "year" INTEGER /* Year of the economic data entry */,
  "country_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the countries table */,
  "indicator_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the indicators table */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the economic indicator */,
  "source_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the data sources table */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the data was last updated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username chosen by the user */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., researcher, admin) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "collaborations" (
  "collaboration_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each collaboration */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the economic data table */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the users table */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions granted to the user for the data (e.g., read-only, edit) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("collaboration_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_versions" (
  "version_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each data version */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the economic data table */,
  "version_number" INTEGER /* Version number of the data */,
  "version_date" TEXT /* Date and time the data was last updated */,
  "changes" TEXT /* Description of changes made to the data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("version_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the economic data table */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., visualization, summary statistics) */,
  "report_date" TEXT /* Date and time the report was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "visualizations" (
  "visualization_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each visualization */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the reports table */,
  "visualization_type" TEXT /* Type of visualization (e.g., line chart, bar chart) */,
  "data" TEXT /* Data used to generate the visualization */,
  "visualization_date" TEXT /* Date and time the visualization was generated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("visualization_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "summary_statistics" (
  "statistic_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each statistic */,
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key referencing the reports table */,
  "statistic_name" TEXT /* Name of the statistic (e.g., mean, median) */,
  "value" REAL /* Value of the statistic */,
  "statistic_date" TEXT /* Date and time the statistic was calculated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("statistic_id")
)
